root@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app# cat > convert_hex.py << 'EOF'
> #!/usr/bin/env python3
> import sys
>
> # Read the hexdump file
> with open('data.hex', 'r') as f:
>     lines = f.readlines()
>
> # Convert octal hexdump back to binary
> binary_data = []
> for line in lines:
>     # Skip empty lines
>     if not line.strip():
>         continue
>
>     # Split line and skip address column
>     parts = line.strip().split()
>     if len(parts) < 2:
>         continue
>
>     # Convert octal values to bytes (skip address)
>     for octal_val in parts[1:]:
>         try:
>             # Convert octal to integer, then to 16-bit little-endian bytes
>             val = int(octal_val, 8)
>             binary_data.append(val & 0xFF)           # Low byte
>             binary_data.append((val >> 8) & 0xFF)    # High byte
>         except ValueError:
>             continue
>
> # Write binary data
> with open('data.bin', 'wb') as f:
>     f.write(bytes(binary_data))
>
> print(f"Converted {len(binary_data)} bytes")
> EOF
